content_preview.title: Fervo Energy Raises $1.89 Billion in Nasdaq Debut, Shares Jump 33% as Geothermal Bets on AI Data Center Demand
content_preview.summary: Fervo Energy priced 70 million shares at $27 each on May 13, surging 33% on its first trading day to push its market cap past $10 billion.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Fervo Energy, the Houston-based enhanced geothermal developer, raised $1.89 billion in its Nasdaq debut on May 13, pricing 70 million Class A shares at $27 each before the stock surged 33% on its first day of trading to push its market capitalization above $10 billion, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/13/geothermal-startup-fervo-energy-pops-33-in-ipo-debut-fueled-by-ai-data-center-demand/). The listing, previously reported as a filing in April, marks the firs...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent, comprehensive News article on Fervo Energy's IPO. Covers the offering mechanics, first-day trading, project pipeline (Cape Station), technology, cost trajectory, financial picture, risk factors, and competitive context. The Analysis section adds genuine editorial value.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (Fervo official press release via Archive.org): confirms 70 million shares at $27, upsized from 55.5M shares, J.P. Morgan/BofA/RBC/Barclays as joint bookrunners, May 13 trading start under FRVO, underwriter option for 10.5M additional shares. source-1.html.gz (The Spokesman-Review/NYT): confirms $1.89B raised, stock opened at $36 closed at $36.54 (33% pop), Tim Latimer quote verified verbatim, Kate Adie/Wood MacKenzie quote verified verbatim, 0.4% geothermal share of US generation, 3,800 MW US conventional capacity. source-2.html.gz (TechCrunch): confirms $1.89B IPO raised, $27 price, 33% first-day pop, $10B+ market cap, Sarah Jewett quote 'We're repeating the playbook from the shale energy industry but with the answer key' confirmed verbatim, AI/data center demand framing, two-thirds cost reduction after 14 wells. source-3.html.gz (Cleantech Group): confirms $36.54 closing price, $7.65B fully diluted valuation at pricing, December 2025 Series E at $2.86B, Cape Station 500MW design/2GW permitted/4GW potential, $138K 2025 revenue and $58M net loss, $7.2B contracted revenue, $421M March 2026 non-recourse financing, 70% drilling speed improvement and 75% well cost reduction to $4.8M per well, $7,000/kW current and $3,000/kW target costs, Google right of first refusal through March 2028. source-4.html.gz (CarbonCredits.com - May 6 pre-IPO article): provides SEC filing quote about 'repeatable product' and '50-megawatt ORC units' — correctly used for filing language, not post-IPO figures.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. The CarbonCredits.com article is a pre-IPO piece ($1.33B projected) used only for the SEC filing quotes — this is correct attribution. The Cleantech Group is correctly cited for first-day trading price and valuation details. All executive quotes confirmed verbatim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Risk factors (thermal drawdown, Google non-binding commitment, unprofitability) are accurately presented. No promotional language.
editor_notes.originality: Prior articles covered Turboden deal (April 8) and S-1 filing (April 28). This article covers the IPO execution — original and non-duplicate.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding News article with meticulous sourcing. All 5 sources verified. Automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS triggered by domain allowlist gaps only. Overriding to APPROVE.
